messages.content:
帮我把下面的内容润色成学术专业的英文，用于论文的related work：
Existing reviewer assignment benchmarks face two main challenges: limited scalability and low annotation precision.  为了解决scale的问题, some prior works construct benchmarks with proxy signals~\cite{} or third-party annotations~\cite{} derived from static conference snapshots. 尽管数据集的scale很大，但是却一定程度上牺牲了标注的质量。An alternative paradigm adopts reviewer self-assessment to establish ``gold-standard’’ benchmark~\cite{}, improving label reliability. However, 这种方法收集数据的效率比较低，限制了数据集的scale

Existing benchmarks for reviewer assignment face two major challenges: limited scalability and low annotation fidelity. To address scalability, several prior studies construct large-scale benchmarks using proxy signals (e.g., implicit interactions) or third-party annotations derived from static snapshots of conference data~\cite{}. While these approaches substantially increase dataset size, they often do so at the cost of reduced label accuracy and increased noise. An alternative line of work relies on reviewer self-assessments to build “gold-standard” benchmarks~\cite{}, which can improve label reliability. However, collecting such self-reported relevance judgments is typically labor-intensive and slow, thereby constraining the achievable scale of the resulting datasets.
An alternative line of work relies on reviewer self-assessments to build “gold-standard” benchmarks~\cite{} by 由受访者自己填写自己了解的paper, which can improve label reliability.

An alternative line of work relies on reviewer self-assessments to build “gold-standard” benchmarks~\cite{}, where reviewers explicitly indicate the papers with which they are familiar or qualified to review, thereby improving label reliability.
Can you propose a smooth transition sentence from this benchmark discussion to our method/dataset contribution?
These limitations motivate our work: we introduce a new dataset and construction pipeline that scales to realistic conference settings while preserving high-fidelity reviewer–paper relevance labels.
不同于之前的数据集一直在scalability和precision上做trade-off， we introduce a new dataset and construction pipeline that continuously scales while preserving high-fidelity reviewer-paper relevance labels.

Unlike prior benchmarks that trade scalability for annotation precision (or vice versa), we introduce a new dataset and construction pipeline that scales continuously while preserving high-fidelity reviewer–paper relevance labels.
